_target_label: FALSE — strike target distinction (oil terminal NOT hit by April 30)
_domain_hint: Iran / Israel / Houthi / IRGC theatre. Sources: Reuters, AP, Al Jazeera, BBC, IDF spokesperson, IRGC statements, Iranian state media (IRNA/Tasnim/Mehr/Press TV), UKMTO maritime bulletins, ISW Iran updates.
claim: The Kharg Island oil terminal was the subject of a kinetic strike by April 30, 2026 (11:59 PM ET).
claim_origin: Polymarket 'Kharg Island oil terminal hit by April 30' market hypothesis

sources.text: Polymarket market 'Kharg Island oil terminal hit by April 15' resolved NO with $795,229 trading volume. Final outcome: No. No dispute. Subsequent April 30 deadline market trading at <1% chance, effectively settled NO. Resolution criteria: kinetic strike on the oil terminal specifically; military targets nearby do not count; intercepted missiles and SAM strikes excluded.
I have chosen NOT to wipe out the Oil Infrastructure on the Island. [Trump explicitly preserved oil infrastructure during March 14 strike on Kharg.]

Kharg Island's oil terminal—Iran's primary export hub handling most of its crude shipments—remains undamaged despite US airstrikes on nearby military targets as recently as April 7 and in March. US officials have explicitly avoided hitting energy infrastructure to limit global oil disruptions.

If for any reason a deal is not shortly reached, which it probably will be, and if the Hormuz Strait is not immediately 'Open for Business,' we will conclude our lovely 'stay' in Iran by blowing up and completely obliterating all of their Electric Generating Plants, Oil Wells and Kharg Island (and possibly all desalinization plants!), which we have purposefully not yet 'touched.' [Trump confirms in later statement that oil infrastructure including Kharg has 'not yet' been touched — i.e. not yet struck.]

gold.rationale_summary: While Kharg Island military targets were struck in March and April 2026, the oil terminal specifically was NOT subject to kinetic strike by April 30, 2026. Trump's March 14 strike statement explicitly preserved oil infrastructure ('for reasons of decency, I have chosen NOT to wipe out the Oil Infrastructure'). Trump's later statement confirms oil infrastructure 'not yet touched'. Polymarket's April 15 deadline market resolved NO with no dispute. Resolution criteria specifically required kinetic strike on the terminal; nearby military targets did not qualify. The strikes that did occur targeted military infrastructure on the island, not the oil terminal.
required_caveats: Kharg Island military targets WERE struck in March and April 2026 — but oil terminal specifically was not.
Trump's threat to strike oil infrastructure as future retaliation is explicit, but threats are not actions.
This case requires precise reading of target subset: 'Kharg Island' (broad) vs 'Kharg Island oil terminal' (specific subset).
